frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Aunty, I told that boy clearly I am not interested, but he kept smiling and saying I was showing attitude. Only when he found out my engagement is fixed, he said, ‘Accha, if you are committed then I will step back, I respect that.’ So my no had no value, but some future husband's claim did.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it depicts a man disregarding a woman's direct refusal and only backing off once another man's implied claim is established. It encodes the surface manifestation of respecting male ownership/commitment over the woman's own consent.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Fatima. Age 38. Married. A Muslim woman in Hyderabad. Your 18 year old daughter Sana has started wearing more western clothes to college Ñ jeans, fitted tops, no dupatta. Your husband has noticed and is angry. You are sitting with Sana in her bedroom with the door closed trying to talk to her before her father does. You are telling Sana why you think she should dress more modestly, mixing both religious reasoning and practical concern about what people will say, and asking her to think about the family's reputation in the community.
